title: PUT and DELETE return success for non-existent IDs (no 404)
evidence: internal/model/book.go:92-99 UpdateBook and :101-108 DeleteBook ignore result.RowsAffected and always return nil, so book_handler.go:141 and :165 'book not found' 404 branches are unreachable — PUT /books/{missing} returns 200, DELETE /books/{missing} returns 204
suggestion: Check RowsAffected in UpdateBook/DeleteBook and return errors.New("book not found") when 0 so the handler emits 404

title: PUT response body has id=0 and zero timestamps
evidence: internal/handler/book_handler.go:132-151 builds the response Book from the request without setting book.ID = id or reloading, so the JSON returned on update has id=0 and empty created_at/updated_at
suggestion: Set book.ID = id (and reload or set UpdatedAt) before encoding the update response

title: Error responses labelled text/plain despite JSON body
evidence: internal/handler/book_handler.go:31,36,41,53,67,74,98,118,123,128,145,159,169 use http.Error, which sets Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8 while writing a JSON error body
suggestion: Write error responses with w.Header().Set("Content-Type","application/json") + w.WriteHeader + json.Encode for consistent JSON content type (R8)

title: Coverage 33.7% — author filter, list, and update paths untested
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.337; book_handler_test.go has no test for ListBooks/?author= (R3) or UpdateBook (R5), and internal/model + internal/migrate + cmd/main have no tests
suggestion: Add tests exercising GET /books?author=, GET /books, and PUT /books/{id} (success + missing) to cover R3/R5 and the store layer
